data.title: Chipotle Mexican Grill Inc. Announces Decline In Q1 Bottom Line
data.description:
WASHINGTON (dpa-AFX) - Chipotle Mexican Grill Inc. (CMG-B) reported a profit for first quarter that Drops, from last year

The company's bottom line came in at $302.82 million, or $0.23 per share. This compares with $386.59 million, or $0.28 per share, last year.

Excluding items, Chipotle Mexican Grill Inc. reported adjusted earnings of $316.22 million or $0.24 per share for the period.

The company's revenue for the period rose 7.4% to $3.088 billion from $2.875 billion last year.

Chipotle Mexican Grill Inc. earnings at a glance (GAAP) :

-Earnings: $302.82 Mln. vs. $386.59 Mln. last year.

-EPS: $0.23 vs. $0.28 last year.

-Revenue: $3.088 Bln vs. $2.875 Bln last year.

data.title: Key facts: Vale Q1 profit up 36%; mines to resume; 2026 payout mix
data.description:
• VALE3 Q1: net profit $1.89B (+36% YoY), revenue $9.26B (+14%), EBITDA ~$3.8–3.9B (+21–23%). Higher volumes, stronger iron ore/copper/nickel prices, record quarterly production; free cash flow +61%.12
  • VALE3 expects Fabrica and Viga mines in Minas Gerais to resume operations within weeks after a January water overflow; CFO says the incident won't materially alter 2026 iron ore production guidance.3
  • Vale plans to shift shareholder returns in 2026, aiming for a more balanced mix between buybacks and dividends versus 2025; this signals a change in cash allocation to investors.4

data.title: Google Reports 81% Jump in Profits, Beating Wall Street Expectations — WSJ
data.description:
By Katherine Blunt

Google parent Alphabet on Wednesday reported a 22% increase in first-quarter revenue.

Sales exceeded analyst expectations at about $110 billion. Net income was $62.6 billion, an 81% increase compared with the same period a year earlier.

The company reported a record $403 billion in sales in 2025. Profits reached about $132 billion last year.

Like other tech giants, Alphabet is spending heavily to advance its AI models and build data centers needed to train and run them. Earlier this year, Alphabet lifted estimates for capital expenditures in 2026 to $175 billion to $185 billion, up from $91 billion to $93 billion in 2025.

Google's cloud division, which sells computing power to data centers, has grown significantly as a result of the AI race. The cloud unit had $20 billion in first-quarter revenue, a 63% increase from the same period in 2025.

Write to Katherine Blunt at katherine.blunt@wsj.com

data.title: Canada's Winpak Q1 revenue misses estimates on weak customer demand
data.description:
Overview

  • Canada packaging materials maker's Q1 revenue fell 1.7% yr/yr, missing analyst expectations

  • Q1 net income attributable to equity holders declined 10.4% from prior year

  • Lower gross profit and weaker sales volumes contributed to earnings decline

Outlook

  • Company forecasts 2026 capital expenditures between $80 mln and $90 mln

  • Company expects new business and added extrusion capacity to drive growth from mid-2026

Result Drivers

  • WEAKER CUSTOMER DEMAND - Co said sales volumes fell 2.1% yr/yr due to weakened demand in several product categories and prior-year order shifts related to tariffs

  • GROSS PROFIT MARGIN PRESSURE - Gross profit margins declined due to slightly higher raw material costs and selling price concessions over the past year

  • SEGMENT VARIATION - Flexible packaging and packaging machinery volumes declined, while specialized printed packaging saw strong growth from nutraceutical business gains

data.title: Cimpress PLC reports results for the quarter ended March 31 - Earnings Summary
data.description:
• Cimpress PLC reported quarterly adjusted earnings of 55 cents​​ per share for the quarter ended March 31, lower than the same quarter last year, when the company reported EPS of $2.36. The mean expectation of two analysts for the quarter was for earnings of $1.64 per share. Wall Street expected results to range from 14 cents to 21 cents per share.

  • Revenue rose 32% to $1.04 billion from a year ago; analysts expected $993.75 million.

  • Cimpress PLC's reported EPS for the quarter was 55 cents​.

  • The company reported quarterly net income of $13.84 million.

  • Cimpress PLC shares had risen by 13.3% this quarter and gained 24.2% so far this year.

FORECAST CHANGES

  • The mean earnings estimate of analysts had fallen by about 55.1% in the last three months.​

  • In the last 30 days, there have been no earnings estimate revisions by analysts covering the company.

RECOMMENDATIONS

  • The current average analyst rating on the shares is "strong buy" and the breakdown of recommendations is 2 "strong buy" or "buy," no "hold" and no "sell" or "strong sell." The average consensus recommendation for the commercial printing services peer group is "buy."

  • Wall Street's median 12-month price target for Cimpress PLC is $97.50, about 15.2% above its last closing price of $82.68

This summary was machine generated from LSEG data April 29 at 09:05 p.m. UTC. ​All figures in US dollars unless otherwise stated. data.title: Landmark Bancorp Q1 net interest income rises on higher yields on loans
data.description:
Overview

  • US community bank's Q1 EPS rose 6.7% yr/yr to $0.83

  • Q1 net interest income grew 14.5% yr/yr, driven by higher rates and lower funding costs

  • Company declared quarterly dividend of $0.21 per share, up from $0.20 prior quarter

Outlook

  • Company did not provide specific guidance or outlook for future quarters in its press release

Result Drivers

  • NET INTEREST INCOME - Growth driven by higher yields on loans and investments and lower funding costs

  • DEPOSIT COSTS - Deposit costs declined due to lower rates on money market, checking accounts, and certificates of deposit

  • NON-INTEREST EXPENSES - Decrease from prior quarter due to lower compensation and benefits and reduced valuation allowances on repossessed assets

data.title: Amazon Earnings Beat Estimates. The Stock Is Falling Anyway. — Barrons.com
data.description:
By Angela Palumbo

Amazon.com investors will have a lot to look for when the e-commerce giant reports earnings on Wednesday, including cloud growth and impacts from the war in Iran.

Amazon is scheduled to report first-quarter earnings after the stock market closes. Analysts surveyed by FactSet expect the company will post adjusted earnings of $1.63 a share, which would be up from earnings of $1.59 a share in the same period a year ago. Revenue for the quarter is estimated to be $177.3 billion, a rise from last year's $155.7 billion.

Amazon is spending big on artificial intelligence, and that's worried investors. The stock dropped after the company reported fourth-quarter financials in February in response to management officials announcing they expect to spend $200 billion on AI in 2026. Shareholders will be watching closely to see if Amazon plans to add to those estimates.

First-quarter purchases of property and equipment, one metric to watch to help gauge Amazon's capital spending, is expected to be $43.6 billion, a jump from last year's $25 billion.

Amazon Web Services is the company's cloud segment. Revenue growth there is key as investors look for proof that companies are seeing returns on their massive AI spending, which is impacting free cash flows. AWS revenue is expected to be $36.6 billion, a 25% jump from the prior year.

Wall Street will also be looking for updates on Amazon's partnership with another AI start-up, OpenAI. Microsoft and OpenAI said in a joint statement on Monday that they no longer have an exclusive partnership. Then on Tuesday, Amazon and OpenAI announced an expansion to their own partnership.

Amazon is also the world's largest online retailer. Shareholders closely watch consumer spending patterns to see if there will be an impact on Amazon sales. The war in Iran has accelerated concerns about consumer sentiment as oil prices rise, impacting inflation and potentially leading to higher for longer interest rates. Data show that consumer spending is still healthy, though. The rising cost of oil, however, could have an impact on Amazon's margins.

"While our e-commerce estimates remain unchanged we contemplate higher operating (namely shipping) costs," UBS analyst Stephen Ju wrote on April 23. He rates Amazon as a Buy with a $304 price target.

Write to Angela Palumbo at angela.palumbo@dowjones.com

This content was created by Barron's, which is operated by Dow Jones & Co. Barron's is published independently from Dow Jones Newswires and The Wall Street Journal.
